definition: The 1966-67 Vietnam War mid-cycle slowdown is identified as the most operationally useful historical analogue (similarity score 0.72, though scores were refuted as false precision). The explicit sequence was: indicator deterioration → credit mini-crisis → Fed reversal → re-acceleration → inflation acceleration → eventual recession (Dec 1969, 3 years after initial scare). If applicable, the current cycle's resolution window is 2027-2028.
evidence: 1966-67 featured fiscal deficit (3-4% GDP) overwhelming Fed tightening
Leading indicators deteriorated without recession, near-stall GDP (0.2% annualized Q1 1967)
Sequence provides testable operational roadmap with 3-year lag
Judged more operationally relevant than 1946-48 analogue
open_questions: Current 6-7% deficit is 2x the 1966-67 level — does this invalidate the analogue?
No analogue captures maturity wall, covenant-lite, and private credit opacity simultaneously
What are the five specific signposts for real-time analogue tracking?
